revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ishinokarato Kofun]][[ja:石のカラト古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ishinokarato Kofun | native_name =石のカラト古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ishi-no-Karato Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ishinokarato Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ishinokarato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.72375|N|135.7785|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)|1=Nara (city)}}, and {{ill|Kizugawa, Kyoto|en|Kizugawa, Kyoto|qid=Q990352|lt=Kizugawa, Kyoto|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.8th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[:en:Asuka Period|Asuka Period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|round-topped burial mound|qid=Q11358496|1=Joenkahofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|25.72|N|135|46|42.77|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ishinokarato Kofun'''|石のカラト古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Kabutodai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kizugawa, Kyoto|en|Kizugawa, Kyoto|qid=Q990352|lt=Kizugawa, Kyoto|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}} and the Jingū neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/173655|title=石のカラト古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> On the Kyoto Prefecture side, it is referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Kazahahi Kofun'''|カザハヒ古墳|}}.
==Overview== Ishinokarato Kofun is located on a terraced flat area on the eastern slope of the Heijōyama Hills in the northern outskirts of [[Heijō-kyō]], the capital of Japan in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. It has a very unusual design known as a {{nihongo|''Joenkahofun''|上円下方墳||}} in that the tumulus its lower stage is square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|ほうふん||}}-style base upon which a circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style mound is built. The tumulus thus resembles a Chinese-style chest, or "Karato". The tumulus is built of tramped earth, with the lower tier measuring 13.8 meters on each side, and two meters in height. The upper tier is 9.2 meters in diameter and 1.6 meters high. ''[[Fukiishi]]'', consisting of river stones, protect the surface and drainage facilities such as culverts and drainage ditches are provided around the mound, but most of the ''fukiishi'' on the upper stage have been lost. The burial facility is a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opening to the south. The Sarcophagus is similar to that of the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Kofun|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] and is 1.4 by 2.6 meters, with a height of 1.06 meters. The tomb was robbed in pre-modern times so no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} remained, but per an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1979, lacquer fragments (possibly a lacquered coffin), as well as one gold ball, one silver ball, three silver-adorned sword fittings, pieces of gold leaf, and were found inside the Sarcophagus, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery was also found in the passageway.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Ishinokarato Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 8th century, in the [[:en:Asuka Period|Asuka Period]], after the end of the [[Kofun period]]. Although the identity of the person buried here is unknown, it is believed to be the tomb of an aristocrat from around the early Nara period. It has now been restored to its original appearance and maintained as a park. It is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Takanohara Station|en|Takanohara Station|qid=Q7677191|lt=Takanohara Station|1=Takanohara Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|qid=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/203833.html Nara city home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kizugawa.lg.jp/index.cfm/8,28706,36,420,html Kizugawa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
Ishinokarato Kofun [[ja石のカラト古墳]]
